message:
cve_arena(bd-1f35): refresh pthread thread-stress results to all-pass

Regenerates the bd-1f35 pthread thread-stress scenario run (run_id
`bd1f35-thread-stress-v1`, seed 5151) against the current
frankenlibc pthread lifecycle code. All eight scenarios now pass
across both mode profiles:

  strict:   4 expected_pass → 4 observed_pass, 0 observed_fail
  hardened: 4 expected_pass → 4 observed_pass, 0 observed_fail

Previously the run was stuck at 2/8 pass / 6/8 fail because the
non-C-fixture scenarios (fanout_fanin_single, create_join_churn,
mixed_detach_join) were all exiting with code 101 from the harness
binary. Re-running after the recent pthread fixes produces actual_exit
= 0 / errno = 0 for the same scenarios without touching the scenario
spec.

This commit contains only the regenerated artifact tree:
  - report.json — scenario-by-scenario pass/fail, op_counts, timings
  - artifact_index.json — refreshed sha256+size for each artifact,
    generated_utc bumped to 2026-04-23T02:48:25Z
  - trace.jsonl — per-scenario trace records
  - strict/{scenario}/stderr.txt, stdout.txt — new run outputs
  - hardened/{scenario}/stderr.txt, stdout.txt — new run outputs

The cargo_target_dir moved from `/data/tmp/cargo-target-bd1f35` to
`/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc`, reflecting the rch-cached target
path used by the current runner. Nothing in the scenario spec
(`tests/conformance/pthread_thread_stress_scenarios.v1.json`) or the
run-id/seed changes, so this is a clean before→after comparison of
the same experiment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
